Defendant Phl Variable Insurance Company'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ryan H. Opgennorth, esq. (415-433-8000) of Pillsbury & Coleman LLP for Plaintiff.
Jodi K. Swick, esq. (510-628-2194) of McDowell Hetherington LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant PHL Variable Insurance Company'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint:

Defendant PHL Variable Insurance Company's motion to strike portions of Plaintiff Anna Martin's complaint is denied. Plaintiff's bad faith allegations sufficiently support her prayer for relief for emotional distress and punitive damages. (See Jordan v. Allstate Ins. Co. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 1062, 1079 ["if the insurer denies benefits unreasonably (i.e., without any reasonable basis for such denial, it may be exposed to the full array of tort remedies, including possible punitive damages"]; Frazier v. Metropolitan Life Ins. Co. (1985) 169 Cal.App.3d 90, 101, 108 [affirming award of emotional distress damages for bad faith denial of life insurance benefits].)

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
